title: carduus calcareus
intro: Carduus calcareus, also known as the chalk milk thistle or calcareous cardus, is a species of flowering plant in the thistle family native to Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its seeds, which are rich in silymarin, a flavonoid complex that has been shown to have hepatoprotective and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Carduus calcareus include its ability to support liver health, reduce inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Carduus calcareus can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or capsule, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
